relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The heavens and the earth came into wholeness, and the whole living order stood complete.
On the seventh day, the Divine completed the work by resting. Creation itself was sealed with pause, breath, and completion.
The Divine blessed the seventh day and made rest sacred, teaching that holiness is not only making, but also receiving, pausing, and being.
This is the unfolding story of heaven and earth, when the Eternal Divine shaped earth beneath heaven.
Before the field had grown and before rain had fallen, there was no human yet to serve the ground.
A hidden mist rose from the earth and watered the face of the ground, as life prepared itself beneath the surface.
The Eternal Divine formed the human from the dust of the ground and breathed life into the body. Earth and breath became a living being.
The Eternal Divine planted a garden of delight and placed the formed human there, inside a living sanctuary.
From the ground rose trees of beauty and nourishment, with the tree of life at the center and the tree of knowing good and harm also within the garden.
A river flowed from Eden to water the garden, then divided outward into four streams, carrying blessing beyond the center.
The first river wound through Havilah, a land where gold waited within the earth.
The gold of that land was good, and precious substances and stones were held there, showing that matter itself can carry beauty.
The second river flowed through Cush, carrying the waters of Eden through another land.
The third river flowed toward Asshur, and the fourth was Euphrates, marking the garden as a source with many directions.
The Eternal Divine settled the human in Eden to serve and guard the garden. Human purpose began as care.
The Divine command began with freedom: from every tree of the garden, you may freely eat.
But the tree of knowing good and harm was a boundary. To take it wrongly was to enter the path of death and separation.
The Eternal Divine saw that aloneness was not good. The human needed a true counterpart, a helper facing and answering them.
The living beings were brought to the human, and the human named them, learning relationship with the creatures of the earth.
The human named the animals, but no corresponding partner was found among them.
The Eternal Divine brought deep sleep upon the human and opened the mystery of shared substance.
From the side of the human, the Divine built woman and brought her forth as a face-to-face counterpart.
The human recognized her as bone of bone and flesh of flesh: not beneath, not outside, but of the same living body.
For this reason, one leaves the old house and clings to the beloved, and two become one shared flesh.
They were naked together and unashamed. Before fear entered, the body was transparent, innocent, and whole.
